```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  ex2 <- sample(seq(2,25,1),1); c <- sample(6:15,1)
  bound <- min(1,ex2/c^2)
  vals <- round(c(bound,min(1,ex2/c),min(1,sqrt(ex2)/c),1-bound,ex2),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Sabe-se que $E(X^2)=`r ex2`$. Aplicando Markov a $X^2$, qual limite superior se obtém para $P(|X|\geq`r c`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
O evento $|X|\geq c$ equivale a $X^2\geq c^2$. Como $X^2\geq0$,
$$P(|X|\geq c)\leq\frac{E(X^2)}{c^2}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",bound)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S10Q3_Markov_segundo_momento
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
